topics.topic: AI Triage: What the ER Teaches Us About Diagnostic Tech
topics.description: Drawing on 30+ years in emergency medicine, Dr. Camps examines how AI-assisted diagnostics mirror—and sometimes fail—the split-second triage logic clinicians have always relied on. Attendees leave with a framework for evaluating health tech tools through a frontline clinical lens.
topics.audience: Health tech founders, CMOs, digital health product teams

topics.topic: Telemedicine in the Trauma Bay: Lessons From the Front Lines
topics.description: Emergency medicine has quietly become one of the most radical testing grounds for telehealth innovation. Dr. Camps delivers an unfiltered clinical perspective on where telemedicine saves lives—and where it falls dangerously short.
topics.audience: Telehealth executives, policy leaders, digital health investors

topics.topic: Wearables in the Wild: What ER Doctors See That Data Misses
topics.description: Patients increasingly arrive in emergency departments armed with wearable data—and clinicians are navigating a new layer of noise versus signal. Dr. Camps offers a grounded, clinical take on the promise and peril of consumer health wearables.
topics.audience: Wearable tech companies, digital health innovators, clinical informaticists

discussion_points: AI Triage: What the ER Teaches Us About Diagnostic Tech
Telemedicine in the Trauma Bay: Lessons From the Front Lines
Wearables in the Wild: What ER Doctors See That Data Misses
bio: 30+ years frontline ER experience. Published author of Never Let Them See You Sweat. Extensive media presence across TV, print, and digital.
